```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-04-01
5	---
6	
7	# Add GitHub Action to auto-deploy to Cloudflare on PR merge into main
8	
9	Set up a GitHub Actions workflow that runs `npm run deploy` (wrangler deploy) automatically when a PR is merged into `main`. This removes the manual deploy step after each merge.
10	
11	## Considerations
12	
13	- Store `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` and `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` as GitHub repository secrets
14	- Trigger on `push` to `main` (which covers PR merges)
15	- Could also run type-check (`npx tsc --noEmit`) as a pre-deploy gate
16
```
</details></tool-use>

Good, I have everything I need. Here's what I found:

- No `.github/` directory exists yet, so the workflow is net-new
- `npm run deploy` runs both `deploy:api` (wrangler deploy) and `deploy:landing` (vite build + wrangler pages deploy)
- The Worker uses a secret `NASA_API_KEY` (set via wrangler), plus deploy needs `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` and `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID`
- Landing page deploys to Cloudflare Pages project `nasapicture-landing`
- No `.nvmrc` or `.node-version` file exists
- No existing CI/CD workflows
